headline: Hepatitis C Treatment Access Limited to 12% of Diagnosed Patients
context: Despite availability of curative DAA medications, only 12% of diagnosed hepatitis C patients receive treatment.

cards.big: Only 12% of 420,000 diagnosed hepatitis C patients receive curative DAA treatment in 2025
cards.sub: Treatment costs RM14,000 per course versus RM280,000 for liver transplant if untreated

cards.big: Generic DAA medicines available at RM1,800 per course but procurement restricted to branded versions
cards.sub: Patent and procurement policies inflate treatment costs by 680% above generic pricing

cards.big: WHO hepatitis C elimination target requires treating 80% of patients by 2030
cards.sub: Malaysia's 12% treatment rate makes the global target mathematically impossible to achieve

cards.big: Egypt treated 4.2 million hepatitis C patients in 3 years using compulsory licensed generics
cards.sub: Political will to override patents enabled the most successful elimination campaign globally

cards.big: Denying a cure that exists because of patent costs is choosing profit over lives
Should Malaysia issue compulsory licenses for hepatitis C medicines to enable universal treatment
